title: When NOT to use an agent: simpler approaches first

body.concept: Agentic systems trade latency and cost for better task performance. Before reaching for agents, ask: can a single optimized LLM call with retrieval and in-context examples solve this? If yes, stop there. If you need more structure, use a workflow (predefined code paths). Only use a true agent (model-driven decisions) when flexibility is needed at scale.
body.analogy: Like choosing transport: don't rent a helicopter (agent) when a bicycle (single LLM call) gets you there. A bus route (workflow) is the middle ground — predictable, efficient, covers most needs.
body.visual: A ladder with three rungs, bottom to top: '1. Single LLM call + retrieval' → '2. Workflow (predefined paths)' → '3. Agent (model-driven)'. An arrow labeled 'Add complexity only when needed' points upward.
